examples.sentence: Come on, hurry your ass up if you want to catch the bus!
examples.translation: Come on, hurry your ass up if you want to catch the bus!

examples.sentence: I told Jake to finish his homework, and he better move his ass before dinner.
examples.translation: I told Jake to finish his homework, and he better move his ass before dinner.

examples.sentence: If you want this project done by Friday, you'd better get your ass in gear starting now.
examples.translation: If you want this project done by Friday, you'd better get your ass in gear starting now.

examples.sentence: Is your boy coming to the family reunion next weekend?
examples.translation: Is your boy coming to the family reunion next weekend?

examples.sentence: I remember when your boy was just starting kindergarten — time flies!
examples.translation: I remember when your boy was just starting kindergarten — time flies!

examples.sentence: Your boy has really grown up since I last saw him at the summer camp.
examples.translation: Your boy has really grown up since I last saw him at the summer camp.

examples.sentence: If your boy wants to join the soccer team, he should sign up by Friday.
examples.translation: If your boy wants to join the soccer team, he should sign up by Friday.

examples.sentence: Don't worry, your boy will handle the deliveries while you're away.
examples.translation: Don't worry, your boy will handle the deliveries while you're away.

examples.sentence: I’ve been mentoring your boy for a few months now, and he’s really improving.
examples.translation: I’ve been mentoring your boy for a few months now, and he’s really improving.

examples.sentence: If your boy wants to join the team, tell him to come to practice tomorrow.
examples.translation: If your boy wants to join the team, tell him to come to practice tomorrow.

examples.sentence: The traffic was awful this morning—you're telling me!
examples.translation: The traffic was awful this morning—you're telling me!

examples.sentence: She said the exam was tough. I replied, 'You're telling me, I barely finished on time.'
examples.translation: She said the exam was tough. I replied, 'You're telling me, I barely finished on time.'

examples.sentence: ‘It's freezing outside today.’ ‘You're telling me! I forgot my coat.’
examples.translation: ‘It's freezing outside today.’ ‘You're telling me! I forgot my coat.’

examples.sentence: So, you're telling me that we have to redo the entire presentation by tomorrow?
examples.translation: So, you're telling me that we have to redo the entire presentation by tomorrow?

examples.sentence: You're telling me the meeting has been moved up to this afternoon instead of next week?
examples.translation: You're telling me the meeting has been moved up to this afternoon instead of next week?

examples.sentence: If I’m hearing you right, you're telling me we won’t get any extra funding this quarter?
examples.translation: If I’m hearing you right, you're telling me we won’t get any extra funding this quarter?

examples.sentence: I think your girl is waiting for us at the café.
examples.translation: I think your girl is waiting for us at the café.

examples.sentence: Your girl called earlier and said she’ll be late to the party.
examples.translation: Your girl called earlier and said she’ll be late to the party.

examples.sentence: Make sure your girl knows about the change in plans tonight.
examples.translation: Make sure your girl knows about the change in plans tonight.

examples.sentence: Your girl has a dance recital this weekend, right?
examples.translation: Your girl has a dance recital this weekend, right?

examples.sentence: I heard your girl is doing really well in school this semester.
examples.translation: I heard your girl is doing really well in school this semester.

examples.sentence: Your girl asked if you could pick her up after practice today.
examples.translation: Your girl asked if you could pick her up after practice today.

examples.sentence: Don't forget that your girl needs a new backpack for school next week.
examples.translation: Don't forget that your girl needs a new backpack for school next week.

examples.sentence: If you need help moving, just say the word—I'm your girl!
examples.translation: If you need help moving, just say the word—I'm your girl!

examples.sentence: When it comes to organizing events, I'm your girl; I love the challenge.
examples.translation: When it comes to organizing events, I'm your girl; I love the challenge.

examples.sentence: Don’t worry about the presentation; I’m your girl and will handle it.
examples.translation: Don’t worry about the presentation; I’m your girl and will handle it.

examples.sentence: Why did the server crash last night? Your guess is as good as mine.
examples.translation: Why did the server crash last night? Your guess is as good as mine.

examples.sentence: I asked her when the meeting would start, but your guess is as good as mine.
examples.translation: I asked her when the meeting would start, but your guess is as good as mine.

examples.sentence: We don’t know why the package hasn’t arrived yet—your guess is as good as mine.
examples.translation: We don’t know why the package hasn’t arrived yet—your guess is as good as mine.

examples.sentence: The reason for the delay? Your guess is as good as mine.
examples.translation: The reason for the delay? Your guess is as good as mine.

examples.sentence: Who will win the tournament? Honestly, your guess is as good as mine.
examples.translation: Who will win the tournament? Honestly, your guess is as good as mine.

examples.sentence: Will the new policy improve sales? Your guess is as good as mine at this point.
examples.translation: Will the new policy improve sales? Your guess is as good as mine at this point.

examples.sentence: The car’s manual says it gets 30 miles per gallon, but your mileage may vary depending on driving habits.
examples.translation: The car’s manual says it gets 30 miles per gallon, but your mileage may vary depending on driving habits.

examples.sentence: I've heard the hybrid is very fuel-efficient, but your mileage may vary in city traffic.
examples.translation: I've heard the hybrid is very fuel-efficient, but your mileage may vary in city traffic.

examples.sentence: The advertised range on electric cars is often optimistic; your mileage may vary with weather conditions.
examples.translation: The advertised range on electric cars is often optimistic; your mileage may vary with weather conditions.

examples.sentence: While the truck claims 20 mpg on the highway, your mileage may vary if you haul heavy loads.
examples.translation: While the truck claims 20 mpg on the highway, your mileage may vary if you haul heavy loads.

examples.sentence: Many people find this diet effective, but your mileage may vary depending on your metabolism.
examples.translation: Many people find this diet effective, but your mileage may vary depending on your metabolism.

examples.sentence: The app promises to improve productivity, but your mileage may vary based on how you use it.
examples.translation: The app promises to improve productivity, but your mileage may vary based on how you use it.

examples.sentence: This workout plan works great for some, but your mileage may vary if you have pre-existing injuries.
examples.translation: This workout plan works great for some, but your mileage may vary if you have pre-existing injuries.

examples.sentence: Dear Mr. Smith, I look forward to your response. Yours sincerely, Jane Doe.
examples.translation: Dear Mr. Smith, I look forward to your response. Yours sincerely, Jane Doe.

examples.sentence: In formal letters, it’s polite to end with ‘Yours sincerely’ when you know the recipient’s name.
examples.translation: In formal letters, it’s polite to end with ‘Yours sincerely’ when you know the recipient’s name.

examples.sentence: He closed the email with ‘Yours sincerely’ before signing off.
examples.translation: He closed the email with ‘Yours sincerely’ before signing off.

examples.sentence: Well, yours sincerely made a big mess in the kitchen this time!
examples.translation: Well, yours sincerely made a big mess in the kitchen this time!

examples.sentence: I forgot to call you back—yours sincerely apologizes profusely.
examples.translation: I forgot to call you back—yours sincerely apologizes profusely.

examples.sentence: Yours sincerely will be attending the party fashionably late, as usual.
examples.translation: Yours sincerely will be attending the party fashionably late, as usual.

examples.sentence: Thanks for the invite! Yours trulies, Mike.
examples.translation: Thanks for the invite! Yours trulies, Mike.

examples.sentence: She ended her letter with ‘Yours trulies’ to add a bit of humor.
examples.translation: She ended her letter with ‘Yours trulies’ to add a bit of humor.

examples.sentence: I always sign off my emails with ‘Yours trulies’ just to make people smile.
examples.translation: I always sign off my emails with ‘Yours trulies’ just to make people smile.

examples.sentence: He meant to write ‘Yours truly’ but accidentally typed ‘Yours trulies.’
examples.translation: He meant to write ‘Yours truly’ but accidentally typed ‘Yours trulies.’

examples.sentence: The card read ‘Yours trulies,’ which made everyone chuckle at the typo.
examples.translation: The card read ‘Yours trulies,’ which made everyone chuckle at the typo.

examples.sentence: ‘Yours trulies’ became an inside joke after she sent the email with that misspelling.
examples.translation: ‘Yours trulies’ became an inside joke after she sent the email with that misspelling.

examples.sentence: Please find the report attached. Yours truly, Sarah Johnson.
examples.translation: Please find the report attached. Yours truly, Sarah Johnson.

examples.sentence: He signed the letter ‘Yours truly’ to show his sincere regards.
examples.translation: He signed the letter ‘Yours truly’ to show his sincere regards.

examples.sentence: At the end of the invitation, it read ‘Yours truly’ followed by the host’s name.
examples.translation: At the end of the invitation, it read ‘Yours truly’ followed by the host’s name.

examples.sentence: Who forgot to lock the door? Yours truly, I'm afraid.
examples.translation: Who forgot to lock the door? Yours truly, I'm afraid.

examples.sentence: If anyone’s to blame for the mix-up, it’s yours truly.
examples.translation: If anyone’s to blame for the mix-up, it’s yours truly.

examples.sentence: Yours truly will be hosting the meeting tomorrow morning.
examples.translation: Yours truly will be hosting the meeting tomorrow morning.

examples.sentence: Guess who got the promotion? Yours truly!
examples.translation: Guess who got the promotion? Yours truly!

examples.sentence: Yours truly managed to finish the marathon despite the rain.
examples.translation: Yours truly managed to finish the marathon despite the rain.

examples.sentence: Well, it was yours truly who accidentally deleted the files.
examples.translation: Well, it was yours truly who accidentally deleted the files.

examples.sentence: You brought me flowers? You shouldn't have!
examples.translation: You brought me flowers? You shouldn't have!

examples.sentence: Thanks for the help with my move—really, you shouldn't have gone to so much trouble.
examples.translation: Thanks for the help with my move—really, you shouldn't have gone to so much trouble.

examples.sentence: Receiving that surprise gift made me smile. You shouldn't have, but I appreciate it.
examples.translation: Receiving that surprise gift made me smile. You shouldn't have, but I appreciate it.

examples.sentence: You shouldn't have told her about the plan before I was ready.
examples.translation: You shouldn't have told her about the plan before I was ready.

examples.sentence: Honestly, you shouldn't have accepted the offer without asking me first.
examples.translation: Honestly, you shouldn't have accepted the offer without asking me first.

examples.sentence: You shouldn't have made that decision so quickly; we needed to discuss it more.
examples.translation: You shouldn't have made that decision so quickly; we needed to discuss it more.

examples.sentence: You think the new manager will change the office culture?
examples.translation: You think the new manager will change the office culture?

examples.sentence: I’m considering quitting my job—what do you think?
examples.translation: I’m considering quitting my job—what do you think?

examples.sentence: You think it’s a good idea to invest in that startup?
examples.translation: You think it’s a good idea to invest in that startup?

examples.sentence: You think you can beat me at chess? Not a chance!
examples.translation: You think you can beat me at chess? Not a chance!

examples.sentence: You think that story is true? Yeah, right.
examples.translation: You think that story is true? Yeah, right.

examples.sentence: You think I’m going to lend you money again? No way.
examples.translation: You think I’m going to lend you money again? No way.

examples.sentence: You what? I didn’t catch that last part about the schedule.
examples.translation: You what? I didn’t catch that last part about the schedule.

examples.sentence: Wait, you what? Are you saying the event got canceled?
examples.translation: Wait, you what? Are you saying the event got canceled?

examples.sentence: You what? I thought the meeting was tomorrow, not today.
examples.translation: You what? I thought the meeting was tomorrow, not today.

examples.sentence: You what, if you help me move this weekend, I’ll buy you dinner?
examples.translation: You what, if you help me move this weekend, I’ll buy you dinner?

examples.sentence: Tell you what, why don’t you take the afternoon off and relax?
examples.translation: Tell you what, why don’t you take the afternoon off and relax?

examples.sentence: You what, I’ll cover for you at work if you cover my shift next week.
examples.translation: You what, I’ll cover for you at work if you cover my shift next week.

examples.sentence: You what? I’m sorry, could you repeat that last sentence?
examples.translation: You what? I’m sorry, could you repeat that last sentence?

examples.sentence: You what? I didn’t hear you clearly over the noise.
examples.translation: You what? I didn’t hear you clearly over the noise.

examples.sentence: You what? That’s quite surprising news!
examples.translation: You what? That’s quite surprising news!

examples.sentence: You what? Can you say that again more slowly, please?
examples.translation: You what? Can you say that again more slowly, please?

examples.sentence: You wish to join the club? Please fill out the application form.
examples.translation: You wish to join the club? Please fill out the application form.

examples.sentence: If you wish, I can arrange a meeting with the director.
examples.translation: If you wish, I can arrange a meeting with the director.

examples.sentence: You wish to cancel your subscription? Let me help you with that.
examples.translation: You wish to cancel your subscription? Let me help you with that.

examples.sentence: You wish to speak with the manager? She’s available now.
examples.translation: You wish to speak with the manager? She’s available now.

examples.sentence: ‘I’m going to be the next champion!’ ‘You wish!’
examples.translation: ‘I’m going to be the next champion!’ ‘You wish!’

examples.sentence: He said he could finish the project in one day. You wish!
examples.translation: He said he could finish the project in one day. You wish!

examples.sentence: You think you can outrun me? You wish.
examples.translation: You think you can outrun me? You wish.

examples.sentence: You wish to leave early today? That’s fine with me.
examples.translation: You wish to leave early today? That’s fine with me.

examples.sentence: If you wish, you may take the car for the weekend.
examples.translation: If you wish, you may take the car for the weekend.

examples.sentence: You wish to speak now or wait until after the break? Either is okay.
examples.translation: You wish to speak now or wait until after the break? Either is okay.

examples.sentence: You wish to change your appointment time? Let me check availability.
examples.translation: You wish to change your appointment time? Let me check availability.
